name: Short-chain fatty acid production by gut microbiota
summary: Anaerobic fermentation of dietary fibre by gut bacteria to acetate, propionate and butyrate, and the host pathways these short-chain fatty acids feed and signal to.
description:
Colonic bacteria ferment non-digestible carbohydrate to the short-chain fatty acids (SCFAs) acetate, propionate and butyrate in an approximate 60:20:20 molar ratio, reaching 50-130 mM in the proximal colon. Hexoses are catabolised by microbial glycolysis to phosphoenolpyruvate and pyruvate, the branch point for every SCFA route.

**Acetate** is made from acetyl-CoA via phosphotransacetylase and acetate kinase (with substrate-level ATP), and additionally by hydrogenotrophic acetogens that run the reductive Wood-Ljungdahl pathway, consuming H2/CO2. **Butyrate** is built by condensing two acetyl-CoA and reducing the C4 chain (thiolase, 3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase, crotonase, and the electron-bifurcating butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase/EtfAB complex), then released either by butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase (the dominant colonic route, coupling butyrate output to acetate uptake) or by phosphotransbutyrylase/butyrate kinase. **Propionate** is produced chiefly by the succinate (methylmalonyl-CoA) pathway of Bacteroidetes and some Firmicutes — a reductive-TCA branch to succinate followed by the B12-dependent methylmalonyl-CoA mutase and the mmdA decarboxylase — and, in a minority of taxa, by the acrylate pathway from lactate. A third, propanediol pathway (from deoxy-sugars such as fucose and rhamnose) also yields propionate but is not detailed here.

SCFAs are largely absorbed by the host: butyrate fuels colonocyte beta-oxidation, propionate is gluconeogenic in the liver, and acetate is lipogenic. All three act as signals — agonising the free fatty acid receptors FFAR2/GPR43 and FFAR3/GPR41 and (butyrate) HCAR2/GPR109A, and butyrate additionally inhibits host class-I histone deacetylases — linking the microbiome to host metabolism, immunity and epigenetics.

Enzymes are drawn from characterised representative fermenters (Clostridium acetobutylicum for the butyryl-CoA pathway, Roseburia hominis for the terminal CoA-transferase, Escherichia coli for the acetate and succinate-pathway steps, Anaerotignum/Clostridium propionicum for the acrylate pathway); gut Bacteroidetes and Firmicutes carry orthologues of each.

references.title: Formation of propionate and butyrate by the human colonic microbiota
Phylogenetic distribution of three pathways for propionate production within the human gut microbiota
From dietary fiber to host physiology: short-chain fatty acids as key bacterial metabolites
Diversity, metabolism and microbial ecology of butyrate-producing bacteria from the human large intestine
Energy conservation via electron bifurcating ferredoxin reduction and proton/Na+ translocating ferredoxin oxidation
Colonic health: fermentation and short chain fatty acids

compartments.name: Bacterial cytoplasm
compartments.description: Cytoplasm of anaerobic gut bacteria where fermentation and SCFA formation occur.

compartments.name: Bacterial cytoplasmic membrane
compartments.description: Site of menaquinol-linked fumarate reductase and ion-motive-force generation.

compartments.name: Host colonocyte nucleus
compartments.description: Site of histone-deacetylase activity modulated by butyrate.

metabolites.name: Pyruvate
metabolites.description: Central fermentation node produced by microbial glycolysis (EMP) of dietary-fibre–derived hexoses; branch point to acetate, butyrate, propionate and lactate.

metabolites.name: Phosphoenolpyruvate
metabolites.description: High-energy glycolytic intermediate; carboxylated to oxaloacetate to launch the succinate pathway of propionate formation.

metabolites.name: Acetyl-CoA
metabolites.description: Two-carbon thioester; hub for acetate excretion and the condensation start of butyrate synthesis.

metabolites.name: Acetyl phosphate
metabolites.description: High-energy acyl phosphate; substrate-level phosphorylation donor for acetate + ATP.

metabolites.name: Acetate
metabolites.description: Most abundant colonic SCFA (~60 mol%); FFAR2 agonist and systemic lipogenic/acetyl-CoA substrate.

metabolites.name: Acetoacetyl-CoA
metabolites.description: First C4 intermediate of the butyryl-CoA pathway, formed by head-to-tail condensation of two acetyl-CoA.

metabolites.name: Butyryl-CoA (butanoyl-CoA)
metabolites.description: Immediate precursor of butyrate; source of the CoA/acetate transfer that dominates colonic butyrate release.

metabolites.name: Butyrate
metabolites.description: Preferred energy substrate of colonocytes; FFAR3/HCAR2 agonist and class-I histone-deacetylase inhibitor.

metabolites.name: Fumarate
metabolites.description: Terminal electron acceptor of anaerobic fumarate respiration in the succinate pathway.

metabolites.name: Succinate
metabolites.description: Free intermediate of the succinate (methylmalonyl-CoA) propionate pathway; itself an excreted metabolite and host FFAR/SUCNR1 signal.

metabolites.name: Propionyl-CoA (propanoyl-CoA)
metabolites.description: Immediate precursor of propionate in all three propionate pathways.

metabolites.name: Propionate
metabolites.description: Gluconeogenic SCFA cleared largely by the liver; FFAR2/FFAR3 agonist that lowers hepatic lipogenesis and modulates satiety.

metabolites.name: (R)-Lactate (D-lactate)
metabolites.description: Fermentation intermediate; substrate of the acrylate pathway and of cross-feeding lactate utilisers.

metabolites.name: Oxidized ferredoxin
metabolites.description: Low-potential 1-electron carrier; reoxidation (via Rnf/Ech) couples fermentation to ion-motive-force generation.

metabolites.name: Quinone (menaquinone pool)
metabolites.description: Membrane quinone; in gut anaerobes this is the menaquinone pool that couples fumarate reduction to the respiratory chain.

metabolites.name: Dihydrogen (H2)
metabolites.description: Fermentation end product and interspecies electron shuttle; consumed by hydrogenotrophic acetogens and methanogens.

enzymes.name: Pyruvate:ferredoxin oxidoreductase
enzymes.description: Oxidatively decarboxylates pyruvate to acetyl-CoA, reducing ferredoxin; the structurally characterised reference PFOR. Gut Firmicutes/Bacteroidetes carry orthologues.

enzymes.name: Acetate kinase
enzymes.description: Generates acetate + ATP by substrate-level phosphorylation from acetyl phosphate.

enzymes.name: CO dehydrogenase / acetyl-CoA synthase (alpha subunit)
enzymes.description: Signature enzyme of reductive acetogenesis; assembles acetyl-CoA from a CODH-derived carbonyl and a methyl group. Gut acetogens (e.g. Blautia hydrogenotrophica) use the homologous complex.

enzymes.name: Acetyl-CoA acetyltransferase (thiolase)
enzymes.description: Condenses two acetyl-CoA to acetoacetyl-CoA; committed step of butyrate synthesis.

enzymes.name: 3-Hydroxybutyryl-CoA dehydrogenase (BHBD)
enzymes.description: NADH-dependent in vivo (reduces acetoacetyl-CoA).

enzymes.name: Butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase
enzymes.description: Flavoenzyme that, with EtfAB, performs flavin-based electron bifurcation to reduce crotonyl-CoA and ferredoxin using NADH.

enzymes.name: Butyrate kinase
enzymes.description: Generates butyrate + ATP; minority terminal route in the human colon.

enzymes.name: Butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase
enzymes.description: Dominant colonic butyrate-forming enzyme; transfers CoA from butyryl-CoA to acetate, coupling butyrate release to acetate cross-feeding.

enzymes.name: Phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (ATP)
enzymes.description: In the succinate pathway operates in the carboxylating direction, fixing CO2 onto PEP to give oxaloacetate; nucleotide specificity is organism-dependent.

enzymes.name: Fumarate reductase, flavoprotein subunit
enzymes.description: Menaquinol-dependent fumarate reductase; the energy-conserving reductive step that pulls flux toward succinate.

enzymes.name: Propionyl-CoA:succinate CoA-transferase
enzymes.description: Releases propionate while regenerating succinyl-CoA, closing the methylmalonyl-CoA cycle (gene ygfH/scpC).

enzymes.name: Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase
enzymes.description: Cobalamin radical isomerase interconverting succinyl-CoA and (R)-methylmalonyl-CoA; the B12 requirement links propionate output to microbial/host cobalamin status (gene sbm/scpA).

enzymes.name: Methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase
enzymes.description: Epimerises (R)- to (S)-methylmalonyl-CoA; a characterised bacterial methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase (gut Bacteroidetes carry orthologues).

enzymes.name: Methylmalonyl-CoA decarboxylase
enzymes.description: Decarboxylates (S)-methylmalonyl-CoA to propionyl-CoA (gene ygfG/scpB). The gut-propionate marker gene mmdA encodes the homologous, often Na+-translocating, methylmalonyl-CoA decarboxylase (EC 7.2.4.3).

enzymes.name: D-Lactate dehydrogenase (fermentative)
enzymes.description: Reduces pyruvate to D-(R)-lactate, the entry substrate of the acrylate pathway.

enzymes.name: Propionate CoA-transferase
enzymes.description: Activates (R)-lactate to (R)-lactoyl-CoA using propionyl-CoA, releasing propionate. Structure identifier not asserted (UniProt accession not verified).

enzymes.name: Lactoyl-CoA dehydratase, alpha subunit
enzymes.description: Radical (R)-lactyl-CoA dehydratase forming acryloyl-CoA.

enzymes.name: Acryloyl-CoA reductase (NADH)
enzymes.description: Reduces acryloyl-CoA to propionyl-CoA; electron-bifurcating with its ETF in vivo.

enzymes.name: Free fatty acid receptor 2 (GPR43)
enzymes.description: Host Gi/Gq receptor; acetate>propionate agonism drives GLP-1/PYY secretion, neutrophil chemotaxis and colonic Treg expansion.

enzymes.name: Free fatty acid receptor 3 (GPR41)
enzymes.description: Host Gi receptor; propionate>butyrate agonism modulates gut hormone release, sympathetic tone and energy expenditure.

enzymes.name: Hydroxycarboxylic acid receptor 2 (GPR109A)
enzymes.description: Host Gi receptor for butyrate (and beta-hydroxybutyrate/niacin); anti-inflammatory signalling in colon and immune cells.

enzymes.name: Histone deacetylase 1
enzymes.description: Representative class-I HDAC inhibited by butyrate, linking microbial fermentation to host epigenetic (histone-acetylation) regulation.

complexes.name: Butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase/electron-transfer flavoprotein complex (Bcd-EtfAB)
complexes.description: Cytoplasmic flavin-based electron-bifurcating complex that couples exergonic crotonyl-CoA reduction to endergonic ferredoxin reduction, both driven by NADH.

complexes.name: Lactoyl-CoA dehydratase (LcdAB)
complexes.description: Oxygen-sensitive radical dehydratase of the acrylate pathway (activator subunit LcdC omitted).

reactions.name: Oxidative decarboxylation of pyruvate to acetyl-CoA
reactions.equation: Pyruvate + CoA + 2 oxidized ferredoxin -> Acetyl-CoA + CO2 + 2 reduced ferredoxin + 2 H+

reactions.name: Acetyl-CoA : phosphate acetyltransferase
reactions.equation: Acetyl-CoA + Pi + 2 H+ <=> Acetyl phosphate + CoA

reactions.name: Acetate kinase (substrate-level phosphorylation)
reactions.equation: Acetyl phosphate + ADP -> Acetate + ATP + 2 H+

provenance.notes: Net of the multi-step Wood-Ljungdahl (reductive acetogenesis) pathway; the CODH/ACS complex is the signature enzyme. Hydrogenotrophic acetogens (e.g. Blautia hydrogenotrophica) use H2 as reductant, providing a colonic H2 sink and extra acetate.
Enzyme (BHBD, EC 1.1.1.157) is NADH-dependent in vivo; KEGG R01975 captures the NAD-linked mass transformation (KEGG classes that reaction under EC 1.1.1.35).

reactions.name: Butyryl-CoA dehydrogenase (electron-bifurcating, Bcd-EtfAB)
reactions.equation: Crotonoyl-CoA + 2 NADH + 2 oxidized ferredoxin -> Butyryl-CoA + 2 NAD+ + 2 reduced ferredoxin

provenance.notes: Physiological flavin-based electron bifurcation: one NADH reduces crotonyl-CoA, the second reduces low-potential ferredoxin (Buckel & Thauer). KEGG R01175 lists the simplified FAD-coupled half-reaction (EC 1.3.8.1).
Dominant colonic butyrate-forming route; consumes exogenous acetate, so butyrate output is coupled to acetate cross-feeding.

reactions.name: Phosphate butyryltransferase
reactions.equation: Butyryl-CoA + Pi + 2 H+ <=> Butanoyl phosphate + CoA

reactions.name: Butyrate kinase (substrate-level phosphorylation)
reactions.equation: Butanoyl phosphate + ADP -> Butyrate + ATP + 2 H+

provenance.notes: Alternative (buk) terminal route; less prevalent than butyryl-CoA:acetate CoA-transferase in the human colon.

reactions.name: Phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (carboxylating direction)
reactions.equation: Phosphoenolpyruvate + CO2 + ADP <=> Oxaloacetate + ATP + H+

provenance.notes: OAA-forming CO2 fixation that launches the succinate pathway; direction and nucleotide (ATP vs GTP) are organism-dependent.

Anaerobic fumarate respiration; the energy-conserving reductive step. KEGG R02164 is written with a generic quinone (EC 1.3.5.1); in gut anaerobes the carrier is menaquinone and the enzyme runs as fumarate reductase (EC 1.3.5.4).
Liberates propionate while charging succinate to succinyl-CoA, so the CoA carrier cycles within the pathway.
Adenosylcobalamin radical rearrangement; entry to the methylmalonyl-CoA branch and a cobalamin-dependent control point.
Decarboxylation to propionyl-CoA; the alpha subunit gene mmdA is the diagnostic marker for the succinate-pathway propionate producers of the human gut (often Na+-translocating, EC 7.2.4.3).
CoA-transfer that activates (R)-lactate and releases propionate, cycling the CoA carrier in the acrylate pathway (e.g. Megasphaera elsdenii, Coprococcus catus).
Reduces acryloyl-CoA to propionyl-CoA; electron-bifurcating with its ETF partner in Anaerotignum (Clostridium) propionicum.

regulations.mechanism: Acetate (and propionate) agonise FFAR2/GPR43 on enteroendocrine, immune and adipose cells, stimulating GLP-1/PYY release and colonic regulatory-T-cell expansion.
Propionate is a potent FFAR2 agonist; signalling suppresses hepatic lipogenesis and promotes satiety.
Propionate (and butyrate) activate FFAR3/GPR41, modulating gut-hormone secretion, sympathetic tone and energy expenditure.
Butyrate agonism of FFAR3 contributes to intestinal gluconeogenesis induction and neural signalling.
Butyrate is the physiological ligand of HCAR2/GPR109A on colonic epithelium and immune cells, driving anti-inflammatory (IL-10, Treg) programmes.
Butyrate inhibits class-I histone deacetylases in host cells, increasing histone acetylation and reprogramming transcription (differentiation, apoptosis, anti-inflammatory genes).

relations.note: Microbial glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas) of dietary-fibre–derived hexoses supplies the pyruvate/PEP that feeds all three SCFA branches.
Absorbed acetate is activated to acetyl-CoA and used for de novo lipogenesis and cholesterol synthesis in host liver and adipose tissue.
Acetate-derived acetyl-CoA is oxidised in the host TCA cycle.
Colonocytes take up butyrate and derive ~60-70% of their energy from its mitochondrial beta-oxidation, sparing luminal oxygen and reinforcing gut anaerobiosis.
Butyrate oxidation in colonocytes feeds acetoacetyl-CoA/ketogenesis, and butyrate itself is a beta-hydroxybutyrate mimic at HCAR2.
Portal propionate is a gluconeogenic substrate in host liver and intestine (via propionyl-CoA -> methylmalonyl-CoA -> succinyl-CoA -> OAA).
The succinate propionate pathway runs the reductive TCA branch (OAA-malate-fumarate-succinate-succinyl-CoA), sharing intermediates with the host citric-acid cycle.
